title: Self-Correction Loop
constraints: The agent must review its own output before sending the final response
The review step must catch factual inconsistencies and logical errors
The loop must terminate — no infinite revision cycles
hints: Add a second LLM call that acts as a reviewer, checking the first draft for errors
Use a structured review format: list issues found, then either approve or request revision
Set a maximum number of revision cycles (e.g., 2) to prevent infinite loops

starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: Agent sends first draft without any review or self-correction
def answer_question(question: str) -> str:
    prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
        ("system", "You are a knowledgeable assistant. Answer the user's question accurately."),
        ("human", "{input}"),
    ])
    chain = prompt | llm
    result = chain.invoke({"input": question})
    # BUG: No review step — first draft goes directly to the user
    return result.content

# Test: Questions where first drafts often contain errors
print(answer_question("What is the population of Tokyo and how does it compare to New York?"))
print("---")
print(answer_question("Explain how photosynthesis works and what its chemical equation is."))
print("---")
print(answer_question("What were the causes of World War I and when did key events occur?"))
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: Agent sends first draft without any review or self-correction
def answer_question(question: str) -> str:
    agent = Agent(
        role="Knowledgeable Assistant",
        goal="Answer questions accurately",
        backstory="You are a knowledgeable assistant. Answer the user's question accurately.",
        llm=llm,
    )
    task = Task(
        description=question,
        expected_output="An accurate and detailed answer",
        agent=agent,
    )
    crew = Crew(agents=[agent], tasks=[task])
    # BUG: No review step — first draft goes directly to the user
    result = crew.kickoff()
    return str(result)

# Test: Questions where first drafts often contain errors
print(answer_question("What is the population of Tokyo and how does it compare to New York?"))
print("---")
print(answer_question("Explain how photosynthesis works and what its chemical equation is."))
print("---")
print(answer_question("What were the causes of World War I and when did key events occur?"))
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: Agent sends first draft without any review or self-correction
def answer_question(question: str) -> str:
    agent = Agent(
        name="Assistant",
        instructions="You are a knowledgeable assistant. Answer the user's question accurately.",
    )
    # BUG: No review step — first draft goes directly to the user
    result = Runner.run_sync(agent, question)
    return result.final_output

# Test: Questions where first drafts often contain errors
print(answer_question("What is the population of Tokyo and how does it compare to New York?"))
print("---")
print(answer_question("Explain how photosynthesis works and what its chemical equation is."))
print("---")
print(answer_question("What were the causes of World War I and when did key events occur?"))

evaluationCriteria.label: Output is reviewed
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent performs an explicit review step on its draft before finalizing

evaluationCriteria.label: Errors are caught and fixed
evaluationCriteria.description: Factual inconsistencies and logical errors in the draft are identified and corrected

evaluationCriteria.label: Final output is higher quality
evaluationCriteria.description: The final answer is more accurate and complete than the first draft

evaluationCriteria.label: Loop terminates
evaluationCriteria.description: The revision cycle has a maximum bound and does not run indefinitely
